slides.title: Clear up your back — *it's not dirt*.
slides.sub: Body breakouts aren't about scrubbing harder. They're mostly trapped sweat, friction and fabric.

slides.title: Don't sit in sweat
slides.body: Sweat plus bacteria under a damp shirt is the main trigger. *Shower soon after training*, don't lounge in gym clothes.

slides.title: Friction makes it worse
slides.body: Tight synthetic shirts and a heavy backpack rub pores raw. *Loose, breathable cotton* cuts the friction that inflames them.

slides.title: Scrubbing backfires
slides.body: Harsh loofahs and aggressive scrubbing inflame the skin more. *Gentle wash, then leave it alone* beats attacking it.

slides.title: Wash what touches skin
slides.body: Old sheets and unwashed gym gear re-coat your back nightly. *Clean sheets weekly* removes the buildup you can't see.

slides.title: Calm the whole system
slides.body: Skin anywhere settles when you *sleep enough and spike less sugar*. The body isn't separate from the face.

slides.title: Clear skin, *shoulders to back*.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks the sleep, food & water that calm your skin everywhere — not just your face — in one score.

caption:
"Bacne" usually isn't dirt — it's trapped sweat, friction and fabric. 🚿

Shower after you train, ditch the tight synthetics, stop scrubbing, wash your sheets, and calm the whole system with sleep. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health tracks the sleep, food & water that clear skin everywhere → link in bio.
youtube_title: How to Clear Body Acne (It's Not Dirt)
